settings.title: A Five-Course Tasting Menu Arrives on Tuesdays
settings.excerpt: One seating, one menu, built each week around whatever the market gives us.
settings.body:
We have wanted to cook a little more freely, so Saffron is adding a five-course tasting menu every Tuesday night.

There is a single seating and no choices to make. The kitchen builds the menu around what looks best at the market that morning, which means it changes week to week.

Why one seating and no choices

A set menu with a single seating lets us cook the way we cannot during a normal service. We plate every table at once, so dishes go out at the moment they are meant to rather than whenever each order lands. And because there are no substitutions to juggle, the kitchen can put its full attention into five courses instead of forty. It is a more relaxed night for us, and a more considered meal for you.

What you eat depends on the morning. If the market has the first soft herbs of spring or a glut of stone fruit, that finds its way onto the plate. No two Tuesdays are quite the same, which is exactly the point.

What to know before booking

  • One seating, five courses, no choices to make.
  • Sixty-five per person, with a wine pairing for thirty more.
  • The menu changes weekly with whatever the market gives us.

It runs sixty-five per person, with a wine pairing available for thirty more. Seats are limited and tend to book out, so reserve early in the week. Let us know about allergies when you book and we will plan around them rather than swapping dishes on the night.
